data.title: Market Chatter: Saudi Aramco to Boost Oil Supply to China in May
data.description:
Saudi Arabian Oil (SASE:2222), d/b/a Saudi Aramco, is set to boost its crude supply to China to 48 million barrels in May from 35.5 million barrels in April, driven by sharp price cuts, Reuters reported Friday, citing sources.

The increase is likely to be the highest monthly volume since 2024 and the first rise in Saudi allocations to China in 2025. Saudi Arabia remains China's second-largest crude oil supplier after Russia.

Saudi Aramco did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Lithium Argentina, Ganfeng to Develop Pozuelos-Pastos Grandes Basins in Argentina
data.description:
Lithium Argentina on Friday said it plans to develop the Pozuelos-Pastos Grandes basins in Argentina with Ganfeng Lithium.

The area to be developed includes Ganfeng's Pozuelos-Pastos Grandes project, the Pastos Grandes project (85% owned by Lithium Argentina and 15% by Ganfeng) and Sal de la Puna project (65% owned by Lithium Argentina and 35% by Ganfeng).

The companies are working on a framework to consolidate the three projects and finalize the regional development plan on the combined resources, targeting up to 150,000 tonnes per annum of lithium carbonate equivalent (LCE). The plan will also consider the production of lithium chloride to provide added flexibility for use in battery markets.

A 5,000 tpa demonstration plant is currently being manufactured for installation at the nearby Cauchari-Olaroz lithium operation that is jointly managed by the two companies.

Ganfeng and Lithium Argentina are also exploring financing options, including collaborating with potential customers and strategic partners for offtake and minority ownership interests.

data.title: Texas Instruments falls as US-based manufacturing exposes it to higher tariffs
data.description:
** Shares of chipmaker Texas Instruments fall 3.2% to $151.66 premarket

** The Chinese Semiconductor Industry Association exempts U.S. chipmakers that outsource manufacturing from China's retaliatory tariffs

** The move will benefit chip firms like Qualcomm and AMD that outsource to TSMC, shielding them from the tariff scuffle between the two largest economies

** The CSIA rules the country of origin is where the wafer fabrication plant is located, making TI, with its U.S.-based fabricators, liable for tariff rates of 84% or higher, according to EETop, an information platform and forum for Chinese chipmakers

** As of last close, TXN dowm 16.5% YTD

data.title: Shanghai Electric Plans Up to 300 Million Yuan Share Buyback
data.description:
Shanghai Electric Group plans to launch a buyback program to repurchase A shares worth 150 million yuan and 300 million yuan.

The repurchase plan will be valid for three months following approval from shareholders, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

data.title: Ford, GM, Stellantis Face $41.9 Billion Higher Cost From Tariffs, CAR Report Says
data.description:
Ford Motor , General Motors , and Stellantis are facing $41.9 billion in increased costs from the 25% US auto tariffs, according to a report from the Center for Automotive Research or CAR.

CAR's report also said US President Donald Trump's tariffs on imported auto parts and light vehicles will lead to $107.7 billion in increased costs for all US automakers.

According to the report, the tariffs will impact 6.8 million vehicles produced by Stellantis, Ford, and General Motors.

Stellantis, Ford, and General Motors did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' requests for comments.

data.title: These GameStop directors have followed Ryan Cohen's lead. The stock is rising.
data.description:
By James Rogers

GameStop directors Alain Attal and Larry Cheng have increased their stakes in the videogame retailer and original meme stock

Two GameStop Corp. directors increased their stakes in the video game retailer, just a week after Chief Executive Ryan Cohen grew his position in the original meme stock.

On Thursday, GameStop's (GME) Lead Independent Director Alain Attal purchased 10,000 shares at $25.75 each, bringing his stake to 572,464 shares, according to an SEC filing. On the same day, his fellow director Larry Cheng bought 5,000 shares at $21.54 each, bringing his stake in the company to 83,000 shares, according to a filing.

Last week, Cohen purchased 500,000 shares at $21.55 each on the open market, according to an SEC filing. The purchase took Cohen's GameStop stake to 37,347,842 shares, or approximately 8.4% of the shares outstanding.

Related: GameStop's stock is climbing again. This is why.

GameStop shares are up 2% in premarket trades after ending Thursday's session down 1.5%. The stock is up 132% in the last 12 months.

The company has had a busy few weeks. GameStop recently added bitcoin (BTCUSD) to its investment policy as a treasury reserve asset after weeks of buzz around the benchmark cryptocurrency. GameStop also announced a proposed private offering of $1.3 billion in convertible senior notes. Last week, GameStop said it had completed the offering and exercised an option to purchase up to an additional $200 million aggregate principal amount of notes.

GameStop, like movie-theater chain AMC Entertainment Holdings Inc. (AMC), was a major beneficiary of the 2021 meme-stock frenzy, which sent the companies' shares skyrocketing. However, GameStop has wrestled with declining sales in recent years amid intense competition from digital gaming and streaming.

Related: GameStop finally makes its bitcoin move, and investors seem fired up

The videogame retailer's stock was boosted last year with the return of influential trader Keith Gill, also known as Roaring Kitty, to social media. Gill, a pivotal figure in the 2021 meme-stock explosion, described himself as a GameStop "believer" in June, in his first YouTube livestream in three years.

-James Rogers

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Gold Futures Set New Record on Market Uncertainty, ETF InflowsMarket Talk
data.description: Gold futures rise to a new record high on significant levels of market uncertainty. Futures are up 1.8% at $3,233.80 a troy ounce, having risen as high as $3,255.90/oz earlier in the session. The price looks well-supported given market turmoil caused by U.S. tariff policy, Commerzbank analysts say in a note. The German bank raises its year-end price forecast for spot gold to $3,000/oz, from $2,850/oz. Gold ETFs posted strong inflows of 92 metric tons in March, bringing first-quarter inflows to a three-year high of 226 tons. The largest inflows were recorded by U.S.-listed ETFs, though European and Asian ETFs also registered inflows. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1140 GMT - BNP Paribas is set to be a big beneficiary from a more integrated European banking sector, Jefferies says in a research note. The French bank's repositioning through recent deals in asset management in France and private banking in Germany should drive growth in the next few years while consuming minimal capital, analyst Joseph Dickerson and associate Theo Massing write. There is scope for BNP to play a key part in the European private banking consolidation, they say. Recent elevated volatility should also be good for its strong corporate and investment bank franchise, they add. "We believe that the market is not valuing better [return on tangible equity] prospects and the synergies afforded by asset gathering businesses and the fact that bank is well positioned for stronger European capital markets," they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1130 GMT - Big banks kick off earnings season with 1Q reports that warn of economic turbulence ahead. JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon says potential tailwinds from tax reform and deregulation from the Trump administration are weighing against potential headwinds from tariffs, a global trade war, ongoing inflation, high fiscal deficits and high volatility in the markets. Bank of New York Mellon CEO Robin Vince says the bank is preparing for a wide range of macroeconomic and market scenarios as the operating environment becomes more uncertain. CEO Larry Fink of BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, says uncertainty and anxiety around the economy are dominating client conversations. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1055 GMT - Gerresheimer's results aren't strong enough for a re-rating, Berstein analyst Delphine Le Louet says in a research note. The German supplier of packaging systems posted an adjusted Ebitda margin in line with expectations, but the EBIT margin decline to 2.9% is hitting free cash flow, Le Louet says. This is holding back the company's deleveraging, she says. Shares trade 1.9% higher at 54.85 euros. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

1047 GMT - There is positive read-across for Aegon from Bain Capital taking a 9.9% stake in Lincoln Financial, which is a key peer to the U.S. business of the Belgian insurer, UBS says in a research note after the transaction and its considerable premium were announced. Aegon's group valuation could have an upside of more than 10% if the valuation of Aegon US is increased in line with the 25% premium paid by Bain for Lincoln's average price, analysts calculate. "Given private markets recognize higher valuation for U.S. life insurance companies, we believe Aegon can look to offload some of its U.S. noncore liabilities, where the company has a bias to exit," they say. Aegon shares trade 0.7% lower at 439.7 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1042 GMT - Indian shares ended higher, supported by bank and steel stocks. Investors closely monitored for further developments of Trump's tariff hikes on other countries. China raised its tariffs of U.S. goods to 125% from 84% on Friday, saying they will not respond to any further tariff increases. Among the top gainers, Tata Steel was up 4.9% and Kotak Bank rose 2.85%. Bajaj Finserv rose 2.6% and HDFC Bank gained 2.3%. Meanwhile, Asian Paint led losses, down 0.8% and TCS was down 0.4%. The benchmark Sensex closed 1.8% higher at 75157.26. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

1024 GMT - Raiffeisen Bank International's investment case is solid and shares look undervalued, Erste Group says in a research note. The Austrian bank is unlikely to materially change its annual guidance at its 1Q results as U.S. tariffs are projected to only have a limited impact on economic growth in Central and Eastern Europe, analyst Thomas Unger writes. "With all eyes on tariffs, the momentum for peace in Ukraine appears to have slowed, but the progress there and Russia's new position in the world are highly relevant for RBI," he notes. Erste raises its rating to buy from accumulate and its target price to 29 euros from 20.6 euros. Shares trade at 21.04 euros, down 1.3% in line with the market. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1000 GMT - BP reported a weak first-quarter trading update, which included an unhelpful increase in net debt, Barclays analysts write. BP's net debt is expected to have increased by $4 billion. The volatile trading environment means a stronger balance sheet would be preferable, they add. The analysts lower their replacement cost operating profit estimates by 8% to $4.9 billion and adjusted net income forecast by 18% to $1.7 billion. Shares trade down 1.7% at 335.65 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0927 GMT - BP's higher-than-expected tax rate in its first quarter will drag on the energy major's net income, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. The analysts cut their quarterly net income estimate to $1.46 billion from $1.82 billion after BP said it expects a 50% tax rate over the period. RBC had expected a 41% tax rate, they write. Refining and trading earnings before interest and tax are now seen at a $43 million loss from a $257 million profit, they say. Shares trade down 2% at 334.45 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0927 GMT - BP's first-quarter earnings from its refining and trading segment are expected to be negative for the third quarter in a row, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. This is despite a small increase in refining margins and better liquids trading, they write. The first-quarter update highlighted weak gas and oil trading with overall weaker production, they add. The one bright spot was BP's customer segment where earnings should rise on-quarter, they write. Shares trade down 2.3% at 333.85 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0906 GMT - Oil prices lose ground as the trade war between U.S. and China escalates. Brent crude and WTI are both up 0.2% to $63.43 a barrel and $60.18 a barrel, respectively, after rising more than 1% earlier in the session. China raised tariffs on U.S. goods to 125% from 84% after the White House clarified overnight that tariffs imposed on Beijing add up to 145%. "The extent to which global demand for oil weakens in the new scenario is also likely to depend on the dampening effects on the Chinese economy," says Barbara Lambrecht, commodity analyst at Commerzbank Research. Meanwhile, the U.S. issued fresh sanctions targeting Iranian oil exports on Wednesday ahead of talks with Tehran over its nuclear program. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0903 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index ended 0.6% lower at 1454.76, following Wall Street's overnight declines, TA Securities analyst Stephen Soo says a in a note. The local market has been volatile this week, as U.S.-China trade tensions escalate and uncertainty over potential tariff deals rise. China has raised tariffs on U.S. goods to 125% from 84% Friday afternoon, saying it will disregard any further U.S. tariff hikes, calling American goods "no longer marketable," according to the State Council's Customs Tariff Commission. Soo pegs the KLCI's support at 1400 and resistance at 1490. Among decliners, CIMB Group lost 3.8% and Gamuda Bhd. was 2.1% lower. Meanwhile, Petronas Chemicals rose 5.8% and Axiata Group added 3.9%.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0852 GMT - French banks are well positioned in an uncertain environment given that the domestic banking market prefers lower interest rates and their business mix beyond the local scope is skewed toward corporate and investment banking as well as life insurance and asset management, UBS says in a research note. It made sense to take profits in banks in a falling equity market amid the tariff turmoil, though the sell-off was much too big, analyst Jason Napier and associate Nicolas O'Sullivan write after the news of the sweeping U.S. levies ignited fears of global economic slowdown. In this context, owing attractively valued banks that are less geared to interest rates is also sensible, they add, pointing to Societe Generale's stock.(elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Jefferies Downgrades Skyward Specialty Insurance to Hold From Buy, Cuts Price Target to $59 From $62
data.description: Skyward Specialty Insurance Group has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $57.33, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Mozambique Inflation Rate Up for 5th Month
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Mozambique rose for the sixth month to 4.77% in March 2025, hitting a new high since November 2023.

Main upward pressure came from prices of food & non-alcoholic beverages (12.08% vs 11.89% in February); restaurants & hotels (6.09% vs 6.20%); miscellaneous goods & services (3.32% vs 2.95%) and recreation & culture (3.18% vs 2.23%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.06% in March, after a 0.52% increase in the prior month.

data.title: Verve Therapeutics Gets FDA Fast Track Designation for VERVE-102
data.description:
Verve Therapeutics said Friday the US Food and Drug Administration granted fast track designation for its experimental VERVE-102 treatment for hyperlipidemia and high lifetime cardiovascular risk to lower low-density lipoprotein cholesterol.

The company is testing VERVE-102 in a phase 1b clinical trial. The study's demographic and initial safety and efficacy data are expected in Q2.

Verve Therapeutics shares were up 4.9% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: Aya Gold & Silver 1Q Output Jumps as Morocco Operations Ramp UpCommodity Comment
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Aya Gold & Silver's production of silver nearly tripled in the first quarter as the company continues its ramp-up of operations at its mine in Morocco, and said it has now moved on to producing the more lucrative semi-pure alloy of silver and gold.

On production:

Silver production came to 1.07 million ounces in the first quarter of the year, up from 366,362 ounces a year earlier.

Aya Gold is increasing its operations at the Zgounder Silver Mine, processing 249,743 metric tons of ore, up from 81,331 tons in the prior year.

Vice President of Operations Raphael Beaudoin said the strong performance comes despite planned shutdowns and seasonal rainfall thanks to increased mine and processing rates.

"The plant is currently operating at design capacity and above, with room for improvement," Beaudoin said.

On outlook:

Aya expects to see recovery improvements throughout the year as plant operations are optimized and as more fresh rock is introduced into the plant.

January was the last month in which silver concentrate was produced at Zgounder, said the company. All produced silver is now silver dore, increasing revenue per ounce produced by about 15% compared with the price of concentrate, Aya added.

Silver dore is a mixed alloy of silver and gold that is often produced during the initial stages of refining precious metals.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Done.ai Group AB signs agreement to acquire Nordic digital agency We Assist to expand SME offering across CRM, websites, and marketing
data.description:
24SevenOffice Group AB

Done.ai Group AB ("Done.ai") today signed an agreement to acquire We Assist, a high-growth digital agency and CRM expert with operations in Norway (We Assist AS) and Denmark (We Assist ApS), specializing in website and e-commerce development, performance marketing and international scaling.

This acquisition marks another important step in Done.ai’s journey to become the go-to one-stop shop for businesses, combining smart technology and hands-on services across the full business lifecycle. By integrating We Assist’s expertise in CRM, website development, and marketing automation, Done.ai further strengthens its ability to offer businesses everything they need, from digital visibility to operational tools, in a single, unified platform. With We Assist onboard, Done.ai significantly expands its presence in digital marketing and CRM, two critical pillars for SME success across the Nordics.

“We Assist has built an impressive business with a clear understanding of SME needs, particularly in digital strategy and execution,” said Staffan Herbst, CEO of Done.ai. “Bringing them into the Done.ai platform strengthens our ability to deliver intelligent tools that help businesses look better, work smarter, and grow faster.”

We Assist founder and CEO Kim Kaasene will join Done.ai’s leadership team as Commercial Director, responsible for scaling Done.ai across all markets.

“I’m excited to join forces with Done.ai to take both We Assist and Done.ai`s vision to the next level,” said Kim Kaasene. “We’ve always focused on giving SMEs access to tools and strategies typically reserved for larger companies - and with Done.ai’s platform, we can now do that at scale. With We Assist’s established international presence, Done.ai will be well-positioned to accelerate the rollout of its platform and services across the Nordic region, supported by We Assist’s expertise in digital scaling and go-to-market execution. ”

Transaction Details

Done.ai Group AB has entered into an agreement to acquire 100% of the shares in We Assist AS for a total consideration of NOK 15.8 million, where NOK 5.3 million in cash to be paid at closing and a NOK 10.5 million Seller Credit which can be settled in either cash or shares by 31 August 2025.

We Assist AS owns 100% of the shares in its Danish subsidiary, We Assist ApS, meaning the transaction includes full ownership of both the Norwegian and Danish operations.

The transaction is subject to customary conditions including final board approval and successful completion of financial and operational due diligence.

Financial Performance and Outlook

 

We Assist generated revenues of approximately NOK 10 million in FY2024, with an EBITDA of approximately NOK 1 million, representing a solid and scalable business model supported by recurring customers and digital delivery capacity. With established operations in both Norway and Denmark, the company is well positioned for continued regional expansion.

Under Done.ai’s ownership, We Assist is expected to accelerate its growth, benefiting from the Group’s broader infrastructure, customer base, and technology platform. The transaction supports Done.ai’s ambition to build a fully integrated digital operating system for small businesses across the Nordics.

About Done.ai

 

Done.ai has entered a new chapter marking its transformation from a traditional ERP vendor into a one-stop shop for modern businesses, offering an integrated suite of AI-powered tools that span the full operational workflow. With a modular, API-first architecture, businesses can manage their entire value chain, from first customer touchpoint to back-end accounting, fully integrated in one automated, end-to-end platform.

Through the integration of embedded financial services such as automated treasury management, payment solutions, spend management, and open banking, Done.ai aims to redefine how businesses manage liquidity and financial operations. These services will initially be launched through an exclusive three-year distribution agreement to 24SevenOffice’s extensive customer base, ensuring rapid rollout and adoption.

Combining deep technological expertise, strategic partnerships, and AI-driven automation, Done.ai delivers real-time financial control, operational efficiency, and unmatched scalability, positioning the company as a leading AI-native fintech platform for the business sector.

Done.ai is headquartered in Sweden and listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market under the ticker 247. The ticker is expected to change to "DONE" following the general meeting on April 10 2025.

About We Assist

 

We Assist is a Nordic digital agency with operations in Norway and Denmark. The company delivers strategic and operational services in CRM systems, website development – including e-commerce, performance marketing and international scaling tailored to the needs of small and medium-sized businesses. With a strong client base and a results-driven approach to digital growth, We Assist is a trusted partner for businesses looking to scale their digital presence. Kasper Bisgaard, who currently serves as marketing director, will assume the role of CEO of We Assist.

 

For further information please contact:

Staffan Herbst, CEO and CFO

Tel: +46 10 490 07 00, ir@done.ai

Certified Adviser

The Certified Adviser to 24SevenOffice on Nasdaq First North Growth Market is Partner Fondkommission.

Address: Lilla Nygatan 2, 411 09 Gothenburg

Telephone: +46 (0)31-761 22 30

Website: partnerfk.com

data.title: Unity Bancorp Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Unity Bancorp Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.13​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 93 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.13 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.11 to $1.14 per share.

  • Revenue rose 12.5% to $28.04 million from a year ago; analysts expected $28.82 million.

  • Unity Bancorp Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.13​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $11.6 million.

  • Unity Bancorp Inc shares had fallen by 2.9% this quarter and lost 9.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Unity Bancorp Inc is $54.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 11 at 11:35 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Low density polyethylene (LDPE) export prices down adjust in the United States
data.description: This week, LDPE export prices drifted lower in the US.An industry source in the U.S. informed a Polymerupdate team member, "US spot export prices for low-density polyethylene (LDPE) experienced a slight decline in the week ending, as market participants noted a slowdown in demand amid evolving global trade conditions. Export distributors reported a significant increase in order cancellations, especially from clients in Asia and the European Union, who are exhibiting heightened caution and hesitance in the market. The sentiment among distributors and buyers has progressively weakened throughout the first quarter, primarily due to increasing uncertainty surrounding tariffs. This instability has considerably affected the demand outlook for US polyethylene for the remainder of 2025, prompting buyers to rethink their sourcing strategies. Market sources indicate that a large share of US polyethylene exports is typically sent to the EU and China. Data from the US International Trade Commission supports this observation, revealing that just over 25% of total polyethylene exports are allocated to these two regions. Consequently, the rising apprehension in these critical export markets is significantly influencing the overall performance of US LDPE.FAS Houston LDPE export prices were assessed at USD 1050-1070/mt, down USD (-10/mt) from the previous week.Spot upstream ethylene prices on Thursday were assessed at the 19.00-19.50 cents/lb FOB U.S. Gulf levels, a sharp week on week drop of (-3 cents/lb).The US ethylene contract price for March 2025 settled at the 35.25 cents/lb mark. This price represents a drop of 1.50 cents/lb from its February 2025 settlement levels."

data.title: Truist Cuts Price Target on OrthoPediatrics to $24 From $26, Keeps Hold Rating

data.title: ArgenX rises on FDA approval for self-administered Vyvgart syringe
data.description:
** Shares in ArgenX are up 3.7% after the Belgian biotech company received FDA approval for syringe version of its Vyvgart drug

** U.S.-listed shares of the company also rise 5.1% to $574 premarket

** The pre-filled syringe (PFS) will be self-admin permitted with no specific training nor monitoring required

** "This achievement is expected to get more new patients on drug and help ArgenX achieve its 2030 vision with 50k patients on drug," Degroof Petercam says

** "We see the approval as the next phase in maintaining Vyvgart's launch momentum," KBC analysts say

** Both brokers see around 4% price increase for PFS, which KBC says "should facilitate smooth access under Medicare Part D"

** Brokerage William Blair says "Patients could increase the frequency of dosing given the convenience of self-administration to achieve more continuous symptom relief"

** Jefferies expects ARGX will see limited impact from tariffs given U.S. manufacturing footprint from its contract drug manufacturer Lonza

** Up to the previous session's close, the shares were down 18.6%​ YTD​

data.title: Mitsui Fudosan Logistics Park to Buy 50% Stake in Logistics Facility; to Divest Two Industrial Properties
data.description:
Mitsui Fudosan Logistics Park agreed to acquire a 50% interest in Saitama's logistic facility iMissions Park for 3.02 billion yen, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on Thursday.

The company also agreed to divest two industrial properties, namely, T&B Maintenance

Center Toyama and T&B Maintenance Center Matsue, for 2.15 billion yen, and 1.65 billion yen, respectively, the filing said.

data.title: i-80 Gold President and COO to Step Down
data.description:
i-80 Gold said Friday that Matthew Gili is stepping down as president and COO.

The company said Gili will remain in the roles until a successor is appointed and following a transitionary period.

Gili has served as i-80's president and COO since the company's inception in 2021.

Richard Young, i-80's CEO, will take over as president after Gili's departure.

"We are grateful to Matt for his committed leadership and the significant contributions he has made during his tenure with the company," Young said. "He has played a key role in advancing the Company's new development plan to build a mid-tier gold producer in Nevada."

I-80's share price gained 4.1% on Thursday to $0.76 on the TSX.

data.title: Berenberg Keeps JD Sports at Buy After Fiscal Q4 Results, Share Buyback Plans
data.description:
Berenberg maintained JD Sports Fashion's (JD.L) buy rating after the British retailer's fiscal fourth-quarter results came in better than expected, with adjusted pretax profit guidance for fiscal 2025 remaining unchanged.

"There is no significant change to our low-end EPS estimates this year (FY 2025E) or next year (FY 2026E). We see FY 2027E (ending January 2027) as a recovery year for sales (+1.8% lfl), with EBIT supported by efficiency benefits. In FY 2027E, the full benefit of the (L)100m share buybacks planned for FY 2026E should come through," analysts said in a Thursday note. "The prospect of total sales growth at around 5% in an average year over the medium term looks attractive to us, in the light of the very low forward P/E of c6x to January 2026E."

However, analysts also noted that potential tariff impacts remain a concern as the company generates 22% of its sales in the US and sources a substantial portion of its goods from several Southeast Asian countries.

The price target of 1.28 pounds sterling on the stock was retained.
